text:
Sweden’s Henrik von Eckermann was in tears after hisOlympicgold medal hopes ended with a huge crash on his horse, King Edward, in the individual equestrian final inParis.

The world No.1, who won his first Olympic gold medal in Tokyo three years ago, was one of the favourites to win the event on Tuesday morning and started his routine well on board King Edward,a horse which was described as ‘little and plucky’ by the BBC’s live commentator Andy Austin.

But after the seventh jump on the course – the first which was over water – King Edward jittered and Von Eckermann attempted to regain control.

King Edward managed to jump two more fences in unconvincing fashion and then drastically veered off course just a few metres from the perimeter fence.

The horse then abruptly stopped by a decorative sign of the Olympic mascot, which sent Von Eckermann flying off his saddle and crashing to the ground.

Both the horse and Von Eckermann emerged unhurt from the crash, but in a tearful interview after the event, the Swede described the incident as ‘the worst thing that has happened to me’.

‘It’s a bit unforgivable on my part… but what should I do? I’m the one who makes it up, it’s nothing more than that,’ Von Eckermann told Sportbladet. ‘It’s a shame.’

Asked if he was in shock, Von Eckermann replied: ‘No, I just feel a shame that I have the horse I have and I’m not successful.’

Explaining the moment which sparked King Edward’s jitters, Von Eckermann claims the confusion was caused after the first water hump.

‘Then we got too high a jump. In a way, we got a lot of power forward and on landing I was already heading to the right and he chooses a little to the left. In this I lose my balance and it makes him wonder what is going on,’ he explained.

Just wanted to show this video of Henrik von Eckermann, the world no.1. Not into horse riding so I have no clue what happened here if anyone can tell me.#Olympics2024Paris#Olympics#Equestrianpic.twitter.com/OomqSBMLbW

‘It goes so fast. You only think when you land where the horse is going so that nothing happens to him. You go to the horse… that he is fine. It’s run so there’s not much to do.’

After getting to his feet, Von Eckermann went over to check King Edward and stroked the horse before leading it away from the arena.

Asked what he said in moment, Von Eckermann replied: ‘What do you say?… Somewhere sorry.’

Von Eckermann, who was choked up towards the end of his interview with Swedish media, added: ‘I have never experienced anything like this.

‘I have broken a leg but in terms of results at this point it is the worst thing that has happened to me. I just want to go home to my son and family.’

title: Olympic showjumper in tears as he explains why his horse crashed during final | Metro News
description: Sweden's Henrik von Eckermann was in tears after his Olympic gold medal hopes ended following a crash on his horse in the equestrian final
